checks.describe: the training workflow carries the CPU limit that is the control-plane protection from the starvation post-mortem, plus the 8 GB smoke profile flags — an uncapped pod is what starved the apiserver (Deep Dive: the starvation post-mortem). Read from the workflow manifest the lab applies
checks.run: F=labs/m11/train-workflow.yaml; if grep -Eq 'cpu: *"?2"?' "$F" && grep -q 'limits' "$F" && grep -q -- '--batch' "$F" && grep -q '256' "$F" && grep -q -- '--max-steps' "$F"; then echo cpu-capped-smoke; else echo cap-or-smoke-missing; fi

checks.describe: the executor RBAC grants workflowtaskresults create/patch — the fix for the phase-Error-while-main-Completed wall, where the executor could not write results back (Deep Dive: reading a workflow's true status). Read from the rbac manifest the lab applies
checks.run: F=labs/m11/rbac-executor.yaml; if grep -q 'workflowtaskresults' "$F" && grep -q 'create' "$F" && grep -q 'patch' "$F" && grep -Eq 'kind: *Role' "$F" && grep -Eq 'kind: *RoleBinding' "$F"; then echo rbac-executor-ok; else echo rbac-executor-missing; fi

checks.describe: the deep-dive documents the full break-glass ladder — kubectl dead, crictl DeadlineExceeded, pkill on the node — as the recovery path when the training pod starves the apiserver (Deep Dive: the starvation post-mortem). Read from the deep-dive page
checks.run: F=site/docs/m11-autoscaling-gitops/deep-dive.md; if grep -qi 'apiserver' "$F" && grep -qi 'crictl' "$F" && grep -qi 'pkill' "$F" && grep -qi 'DeadlineExceeded' "$F"; then echo ladder-documented; else echo ladder-missing; fi

checks.describe: the deep-dive dissects KEDA internals — the external-metrics API path, the generated HPA, and the paused-replicas / scale-to-zero knobs — beyond what the lab applied (Deep Dive: KEDA internals). Read from the deep-dive page
checks.run: F=site/docs/m11-autoscaling-gitops/deep-dive.md; if grep -q 'external.metrics.k8s.io' "$F" && grep -q 'keda-hpa-opsmate-model' "$F" && grep -q 'paused-replicas' "$F" && grep -qi 'cooldownPeriod' "$F"; then echo keda-internals-documented; else echo keda-internals-missing; fi

checks.describe: the deep-dive teaches reading a workflow's true status from containerStatuses (the main container's real exit code) rather than trusting .status.phase, which lied while training succeeded (Deep Dive: phase vs containerStatuses). Read from the deep-dive page
checks.run: F=site/docs/m11-autoscaling-gitops/deep-dive.md; if grep -q 'containerStatuses' "$F" && grep -q 'status.phase' "$F" && grep -qi 'exit code' "$F"; then echo status-reading-taught; else echo status-reading-missing; fi

checks.describe: the deep-dive frames the CPU limit as control-plane protection (requests = reservation, limits = the CFS-enforced ceiling that keeps the node governable), not pod hygiene (Deep Dive: requests vs limits). Read from the deep-dive page
checks.run: F=site/docs/m11-autoscaling-gitops/deep-dive.md; if grep -qi 'control-plane protection\|control plane' "$F" && grep -qi 'CFS' "$F" && grep -qi 'reservation' "$F"; then echo control-plane-framing-ok; else echo control-plane-framing-missing; fi
